title: Getting started with bouquets
text: angular accumulation plot encodes time series turtle-graphics path shared origin. every time step: increase turns heading left θ, decrease turns right θ, change goes straight ahead. every series shares origin angle, paths look similar belong series similar patterns ups downs — regardless absolute values. collection paths radiates origin like stems bouquet.

title: Getting started with bouquets
text: Throughout vignette use simulated set eight groundwater monitoring stations, weekly time series containing shared seasonal signal plus independent noise.
code: set.seed(42) n      <- 104L   # 2 years, weekly weeks  <- seq(as.Date("2022-01-01"), by = "week", length.out = n) season <- sin(seq(0, 4 * pi, length.out = n))  gw_long <- tibble::tibble(   week    = rep(weeks, 8L),   station = rep(paste0("S", 1:8), each = n),   region  = rep(c("North", "North", "South", "South",                   "East",  "East",  "West",  "West"), each = n),   lon     = rep(c(7.2, 8.1, 13.4, 14.1, 10.5, 11.2, 6.9, 7.8), each = n),   lat     = rep(c(53.6, 53.1, 52.5, 52.0, 50.8, 50.3, 48.1, 47.8), each = n),   level_m = c(     8.5 + 0.9 * season + cumsum(rnorm(n,  0.00, 0.18)),     8.3 + 0.8 * season + cumsum(rnorm(n,  0.01, 0.20)),     7.2 + 0.5 * season + cumsum(rnorm(n,  0.02, 0.22)),     7.0 + 0.6 * season + cumsum(rnorm(n,  0.00, 0.19)),     9.1 + 1.1 * season + cumsum(rnorm(n, -0.01, 0.15)),     9.3 + 1.0 * season + cumsum(rnorm(n, -0.02, 0.16)),     6.8 + 0.3 * season + cumsum(rnorm(n,  0.00, 0.28)),     7.5 + 0.4 * season + cumsum(rnorm(n,  0.01, 0.25))   ) )

title: Getting started with bouquets
text: simplest call uses first three columns time, series, value.
code: make_plot_bouquet(   gw_long,   time_col   = week,   series_col = station,   value_col  = level_m ) ## <bouquet_plot>  8 series | theta = 11.1 deg | binding: S5

title: Getting started with bouquets
text: stem_colors flower_colors accept single hex code, vector hex codes, keyword ("greens" / "blossom"), bare column name.
code: make_plot_bouquet(   gw_long,   time_col      = week,   series_col    = station,   value_col     = level_m,   stem_colors   = region,   flower_colors = region,   facet_by      = region,   title         = "Groundwater levels by region" ) ## <bouquet_plot>  8 series | theta = 11.1 deg | binding: S5

title: Getting started with bouquets
text: Use highlight bring specific series foreground; others dimmed pale grey.
code: make_plot_bouquet(   gw_long,   time_col   = week,   series_col = station,   value_col  = level_m,   highlight  = c("S1", "S2"),   title      = "Stations S1 and S2 highlighted" ) ## <bouquet_plot>  8 series | theta = 11.1 deg | binding: S5

title: Getting started with bouquets
text: Use restrict time range without pre-filtering.
code: make_plot_bouquet(   gw_long,   time_col   = week,   series_col = station,   value_col  = level_m,   from       = as.Date("2022-07-01"),   to         = as.Date("2022-12-31"),   title      = "Second half of 2022 only" ) ## <bouquet_plot>  8 series | theta = 26.2 deg | binding: S5

title: Getting started with bouquets
text: normalise = TRUE series receives per-series θ every path uses full angular range independently volatility. aids shape comparison across series different variances.
code: make_plot_bouquet(   gw_long,   time_col   = week,   series_col = station,   value_col  = level_m,   normalise  = TRUE,   title      = "Per-series normalised angles" ) ## <bouquet_plot>  8 series | theta = 11.1 deg | binding: S5 [normalised]

title: Getting started with bouquets
text: cluster_bouquet() adds cluster column data. returns cluster_bouquet object: normal tibble also carries clustering diagnostics. Call summary() inspect .
code: clustered <- cluster_bouquet(   gw_long,   time_col   = week,   series_col = station,   value_col  = level_m,   seed       = 42L )  summary(clustered) ## -- cluster_bouquet summary ---------------------------------------------- ##   Method    : pca_hclust ##   Distance  : euclidean (unused) ##   Seed      : 42 ##   Series    : 8   k = 3   Resolution = 0.50 ##   Mean silhouette : 0.031  (weak structure -- consider more data or fewer clusters) ##  ##   Auto k selection (composite silhouette score): ##     k = 2 : 0.0020 ##     k = 3 : 0.0311  <-- selected ##     k = 4 : 0.0154 ##     k = 5 : -0.0040 ##     k = 6 : -0.0013 ##     k = 7 : -0.0074 ##  ##   Cluster sizes and members: ##     C1  (5 series, mean sil = 0.033) ##        S1, S2, S5, S6, S7 ##     C2  (2 series, mean sil = 0.041) ##        S3, S4 ##     C3  (1 series, mean sil = 0.000) ##        S8 ##  ##   Per-series silhouette widths: ##     S5                    C1   0.062  | ##     S6                    C1   0.035  | ##     S2                    C1   0.031  | ##     S7                    C1   0.029  | ##     S1                    C1   0.011   ##     S4                    C2   0.075  | ##     S3                    C2   0.006   ##     S8                    C3   0.000   ## ------------------------------------------------------------------------

title: Getting started with bouquets
text: plot_cluster_quality() plots composite silhouette score across candidate k values; selected k highlighted pink.
code: plot_cluster_quality(clustered)

what: Plot coloured and faceted by cluster
title: Getting started with bouquets
text: cluster column works directly stem_colors, flower_colors, facet_by.
code: make_plot_bouquet(   clustered,   time_col      = week,   series_col    = station,   value_col     = level_m,   stem_colors   = cluster,   flower_colors = cluster,   title         = "Stations coloured by directional cluster" ) ## <bouquet_plot>  8 series | theta = 11.1 deg | binding: S5

title: Getting started with bouquets
text: lon_col lat_col supplied location map appended right bouquet panel. Pass cluster column cluster_hull draw concave hulls around cluster’s points. Hull fill colour automatically matches cluster’s flower_colors.
code: make_plot_bouquet(   clustered,   time_col      = week,   series_col    = station,   value_col     = level_m,   stem_colors   = cluster,   flower_colors = cluster,   lon_col       = lon,   lat_col       = lat,   cluster_hull  = cluster,   title         = "Colour-matched cluster hulls" ) ## <bouquet_plot>  8 series | theta = 11.1 deg | binding: S5 + map

what: Focusing on the cluster core with hull_coverage
title: Getting started with bouquets
text: clusters spatially dense overlap, hulls drawn points can obscure map. hull_coverage (default 1) restricts hull fraction cluster’s points lie closest cluster centroid. Setting 0.75, example, excludes outermost 25% points computing hull:
code: make_plot_bouquet(   clustered,   time_col      = week,   series_col    = station,   value_col     = level_m,   stem_colors   = cluster,   flower_colors = cluster,   lon_col       = lon,   lat_col       = lat,   cluster_hull  = cluster,   hull_coverage = 0.75,   # hull covers the innermost 75% of each cluster   title         = "Cluster hulls: core 75%" ) ## <bouquet_plot>  8 series | theta = 11.1 deg | binding: S5 + map

title: Getting started with bouquets
text: save_bouquet() wraps ggplot2::ggsave() sensible defaults bouquet aspect ratio.
code: p <- make_plot_bouquet(gw_long, week, station, level_m) save_bouquet(p, "my_bouquet.png", width = 10, height = 8)

title: Getting started with bouquets
text: make_plot_bouquet_interactive() produces plotly figure hover tooltips. Requires plotly package.
code: make_plot_bouquet_interactive(gw_long, week, station, level_m)

title: Getting started with bouquets
text: compact end--end workflow:
code: gw_long |>   cluster_bouquet(     time_col   = week,     series_col = station,     value_col  = level_m,     seed       = 42L   ) |>   make_plot_bouquet(     time_col      = week,     series_col    = station,     value_col     = level_m,     stem_colors   = cluster,     flower_colors = cluster,     lon_col       = lon,     lat_col       = lat,     cluster_hull  = cluster,     hull_coverage = 0.75,     title         = "Groundwater directional dynamics"   )

title: Authors and Citation
text: Max Nölscher. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Nölscher M (2026). bouquets: Angular Accumulation Plots Time Series. R package version 0.1.0, https://github.com/MxNl/bouquets.
code: @Manual{,   title = {bouquets: Angular Accumulation Plots for Time Series},   author = {Max Nölscher},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://github.com/MxNl/bouquets}, }

what: Angular Accumulation Plots for Time Series
title: Angular Accumulation Plots for Time Series
text: bouquets creates angular accumulation plots collections time series. series encoded turtle-graphics path turns left increases right decreases — series share origin turning angle, paths look alike come series similar directional dynamics, regardless absolute values. technique structurally related DNA walk visualisations (Gates 1986; Yau et al. 2003).

title: Angular Accumulation Plots for Time Series
text: Optional dependencies unlock extra features:
code: # Development version from GitHub # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("MxNl/bouquets")

title: Angular Accumulation Plots for Time Series
code: n      <- 52L weeks  <- seq(as.Date("2023-01-01"), by = "week", length.out = n) season <- sin(seq(0, 2 * pi, length.out = n))  gw_long <- tibble::tibble(   week    = rep(weeks, 8L),   station = rep(paste0("Station ", LETTERS[1:8]), each = n),   region  = rep(c("North", "North", "North", "North",                   "South", "South", "South", "South"), each = n),   level_m = c(     8.5 + 0.8 * season + cumsum(rnorm(n,  0.00, 0.18)),     7.2 + 0.5 * season + cumsum(rnorm(n,  0.02, 0.22)),     9.1 + 1.1 * season + cumsum(rnorm(n, -0.01, 0.15)),     6.8 + 0.9 * season + cumsum(rnorm(n,  0.01, 0.20)),     5.4 + 1.2 * season + cumsum(rnorm(n, -0.02, 0.17)),     7.7 + 0.6 * season + cumsum(rnorm(n,  0.00, 0.21)),     8.9 + 0.7 * season + cumsum(rnorm(n,  0.01, 0.19)),     6.2 + 1.0 * season + cumsum(rnorm(n, -0.01, 0.16))   ) )  make_plot_bouquet(gw_long,   time_col   = week,   series_col = station,   value_col  = level_m,   verbose    = FALSE ) #> <bouquet_plot>  8 series | theta = 11.1 deg | binding: Station C

title: Angular Accumulation Plots for Time Series
code: make_plot_bouquet(gw_long,   time_col      = week,   series_col    = station,   value_col     = level_m,   stem_colors   = "greens",   flower_colors = "blossom",   verbose       = FALSE ) #> <bouquet_plot>  8 series | theta = 11.1 deg | binding: Station C

title: Angular Accumulation Plots for Time Series
text: Pass bare column name colour series grouping variable, split facets facet_by:
code: make_plot_bouquet(gw_long,   time_col      = week,   series_col    = station,   value_col     = level_m,   stem_colors   = region,   flower_colors = region,   facet_by      = region,   title         = "Groundwater dynamics by region",   verbose       = FALSE ) #> <bouquet_plot>  8 series | theta = 11.1 deg | binding: Station C

what: Dark mode with rings and step markers
title: Angular Accumulation Plots for Time Series
code: make_plot_bouquet(gw_long,   time_col      = week,   series_col    = station,   value_col     = level_m,   stem_colors   = "greens",   flower_colors = "blossom",   show_rings    = TRUE,   marker_every  = 13L,   dark_mode     = TRUE,   title         = "Groundwater — dark mode",   verbose       = FALSE ) #> <bouquet_plot>  8 series | theta = 11.1 deg | binding: Station C

title: Angular Accumulation Plots for Time Series
text: cluster_bouquet() groups series similarity directional sequences appends cluster column plugs directly make_plot_bouquet():
code: set.seed(7) gw_long |>   cluster_bouquet(     time_col   = week,     series_col = station,     value_col  = level_m,     verbose    = FALSE   ) |>   make_plot_bouquet(     time_col      = week,     series_col    = station,     value_col     = level_m,     stem_colors   = cluster,     flower_colors = cluster,     facet_by      = cluster,     title         = "Series grouped by directional dynamics",     verbose       = FALSE   ) #> <bouquet_plot>  8 series | theta = 11.1 deg | binding: Station C

title: Angular Accumulation Plots for Time Series
text: Supply coordinate columns attach location map alongside bouquet. projected CRS handled via coord_crs (e.g. coord_crs = 3035 ETRS89-LAEA).
code: # Stations spread across Germany north to south, coast to border gw_coords <- dplyr::mutate(gw_long,   lon = rep(c(6.9, 13.4, 9.9, 12.1, 7.5, 11.2, 8.7, 14.8), each = n),   lat = rep(c(53.6, 52.5, 51.5, 48.1, 51.2, 49.8, 47.8, 50.9), each = n) )  make_plot_bouquet(gw_coords,   time_col   = week,   series_col = station,   value_col  = level_m,   lon_col    = lon,   lat_col    = lat,   map_width  = 0.38,   verbose    = FALSE ) #> <bouquet_plot>  8 series | theta = 11.1 deg | binding: Station C + map

title: Angular Accumulation Plots for Time Series
text: Pass cluster column cluster_hull draw colour-matched concave hulls around cluster’s locations. Hull fill colour automatically matches cluster’s flower_colors. Use hull_coverage (default 1) focus hull core cluster reduce overlap clusters dense:
code: set.seed(7) gw_coords <- dplyr::mutate(gw_long,   lon = rep(c(6.9, 13.4, 9.9, 12.1, 7.5, 11.2, 8.7, 14.8), each = n),   lat = rep(c(53.6, 52.5, 51.5, 48.1, 51.2, 49.8, 47.8, 50.9), each = n) )  gw_coords |>   cluster_bouquet(     time_col   = week,     series_col = station,     value_col  = level_m,     verbose    = FALSE   ) |>   make_plot_bouquet(     time_col      = week,     series_col    = station,     value_col     = level_m,     stem_colors   = cluster,     flower_colors = cluster,     lon_col       = lon,     lat_col       = lat,     cluster_hull  = cluster,     hull_coverage = 0.8,        # focus hull on inner 80% of each cluster     map_width     = 0.40,     title         = "Cluster hulls (core 80%)",     verbose       = FALSE   ) #> <bouquet_plot>  8 series | theta = 11.1 deg | binding: Station C + map

title: Angular Accumulation Plots for Time Series
text: series, signed first difference binarised +1 (increase), -1 (decrease), 0 (change). heading step accumulates : h_i = h_launch + sum(d_k x theta) k = 2..angle theta derived even volatile series dataset never completes full loop: theta = 360 / max_over_series(max(C) - min(C)) x ceiling_pct C cumulative sum signed steps series. coordinates follow vectorised cumsum(cos(heading)) / cumsum(sin(heading)).

title: Angular Accumulation Plots for Time Series
text: use bouquets publication, please cite :
code: Nölscher, M. (2025). bouquets: Angular accumulation plots for time series. R package version 0.1.0. https://github.com/MxNl/bouquets

title: Angular Accumulation Plots for Time Series
text: Gates, M. . (1986). simple way look DNA. Journal Theoretical Biology, 119(3), 319-328. Yau, S. S.-T., et al. (2003). DNA sequence representation without degeneracy. Nucleic Acids Research, 31(12), 3078-3080.

what: Cluster Time Series by Directional Dynamics — cluster_bouquet
title: Cluster Time Series by Directional Dynamics — cluster_bouquet
text: Encodes time series sequence signed directional steps (\(+1\) increase, \(-1\) decrease, \(0\) change) clusters series based similarity sequences. resulting cluster column can passed directly make_plot_bouquet() via stem_colors, flower_colors, facet_by, cluster_hull. returned object original data tibble (added cluster column) cluster_bouquet S3 object. Call summary.cluster_bouquet() print structured report cluster quality, sizes, member series.

title: Cluster Time Series by Directional Dynamics — cluster_bouquet
code: cluster_bouquet(   data,   time_col = 1,   series_col = 2,   value_col = 3,   k = "auto",   k_max = 8L,   method = c("pca_hclust", "pca_kmeans", "hclust", "kmeans", "pam"),   distance = c("euclidean", "correlation", "manhattan"),   resolution = 0.5,   pca_variance = 0.9,   cluster_col = "cluster",   seed = 42L,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Cluster Time Series by Directional Dynamics — cluster_bouquet
text: data long-format data frame tibble – format expected make_plot_bouquet(). time_col <tidy-select> time index column. Defaults first column. series_col <tidy-select> column identifying individual time series. Defaults second column. value_col <tidy-select> numeric value column. Defaults third column. k Either positive integer specifying number clusters, "auto" (default) select optimal number maximising composite silhouette score across k = 2, ..., k_max. k_max single positive integer. Upper bound considered k = "auto". Capped n_series - 1. Defaults 8L. method clustering algorithm. One : "pca_hclust" (Default) PCA projection followed agglomerative hierarchical clustering Ward's D2. Deterministic; extra packages required. distance argument apply. "pca_kmeans" PCA projection followed k-means. Set seed reproducibility. distance argument apply. "hclust" Ward's D2 hierarchical clustering directly direction sequences using distance metric. Best short series. "kmeans" k-means direction sequences. Always uses Euclidean distance internally. Set seed reproducibility. "pam" Partitioning Around Medoids. robust outliers. Requires cluster package. distance Distance metric "hclust" "pam". One "euclidean" (default), "correlation" (\(1 - r\)), "manhattan". used PCA methods "kmeans". resolution non-negative number. Composite k-selection score: \(\bar{s}(k) \times \min_c \bar{s}_c(k) \times k^{\text{resolution}}\). Higher values favour finer clusters. 0 = plain mean silhouette. Default 0.5. pca_variance Cumulative variance retained PCA. used "pca_hclust" "pca_kmeans". Default 0.90. cluster_col String. Name cluster column added data. Default "cluster". seed Integer NULL. Random seed passed base::set.seed() k-means initialisation. Ensures reproducible results "pca_kmeans" "kmeans". NULL = seed (non-reproducible). Default 42L. verbose Logical. Print k selection table, chosen k, silhouette score, cluster sizes. Default FALSE.

title: Cluster Time Series by Directional Dynamics — cluster_bouquet
text: original data tibble one additional factor column (named cluster_col, levels "C1", "C2", ... ordered decreasing size) plus cluster_bouquet S3 class. object also carries bq_meta attribute containing clustering diagnostics; use summary.cluster_bouquet() print .

title: Cluster Time Series by Directional Dynamics — cluster_bouquet
text: series length \(n\) represented vector \(n - 1\) signed steps \(d_i \\{-1, 0, +1\}\).

title: Cluster Time Series by Directional Dynamics — cluster_bouquet
text: Plain mean silhouette consistently selects k = 2 two large blobs score globally well even finer structure exists. composite score adds worst-cluster penalty resolution exponent.

title: Cluster Time Series by Directional Dynamics — cluster_bouquet
code: data |>   cluster_bouquet(time_col = date, series_col = id, value_col = gwl) |>   make_plot_bouquet(     time_col      = date,   series_col    = id,   value_col = gwl,     stem_colors   = cluster, flower_colors = cluster,     lon_col = lon, lat_col = lat, cluster_hull = cluster   )

title: Cluster Time Series by Directional Dynamics — cluster_bouquet
code: set.seed(42) n      <- 52L weeks  <- seq(as.Date("2023-01-01"), by = "week", length.out = n) season <- sin(seq(0, 2 * pi, length.out = n))  gw_long <- tibble::tibble(   week    = rep(weeks, 6L),   station = rep(paste0("S", 1:6), each = n),   level_m = c(     8.5 + 0.8 * season + cumsum(rnorm(n,  0.00, 0.2)),     8.3 + 0.7 * season + cumsum(rnorm(n,  0.01, 0.2)),     7.2 + 0.5 * season + cumsum(rnorm(n,  0.02, 0.2)),     7.0 + 0.6 * season + cumsum(rnorm(n,  0.00, 0.2)),     9.1 + 1.1 * season + cumsum(rnorm(n, -0.01, 0.2)),     9.3 + 1.0 * season + cumsum(rnorm(n, -0.02, 0.2))   ) )  # Default: PCA + hclust, auto k clustered <- cluster_bouquet(gw_long,   time_col = week, series_col = station, value_col = level_m)  # Inspect the result summary(clustered) #> -- cluster_bouquet summary ---------------------------------------------- #>   Method    : pca_hclust #>   Distance  : euclidean (unused) #>   Seed      : 42 #>   Series    : 6   k = 4   Resolution = 0.50 #>   Mean silhouette : 0.053  (weak structure -- consider more data or fewer clusters) #>  #>   Auto k selection (composite silhouette score): #>     k = 2 : 0.0063 #>     k = 3 : 0.0011 #>     k = 4 : 0.0533  <-- selected #>     k = 5 : 0.0302 #>  #>   Cluster sizes and members: #>     C1  (2 series, mean sil = 0.069) #>        S1, S4 #>     C2  (2 series, mean sil = 0.090) #>        S3, S6 #>     C3  (1 series, mean sil = 0.000) #>        S2 #>     C4  (1 series, mean sil = 0.000) #>        S5 #>  #>   Per-series silhouette widths: #>     S1                    C1   0.074  | #>     S4                    C1   0.065  | #>     S3                    C2   0.110  || #>     S6                    C2   0.071  | #>     S2                    C3   0.000   #>     S5                    C4   0.000   #> ------------------------------------------------------------------------  # Plot silhouette scores across k to inspect the elbow # \donttest{ plot_cluster_quality(clustered)  # }

what: Format a bouquet_plot for display — format.bouquet_plot
title: Format a bouquet_plot for display — format.bouquet_plot
text: Returns one-line description bouquet plot suitable base::cat() automatic printing interactive sessions.

title: Format a bouquet_plot for display — format.bouquet_plot
code: # S3 method for class 'bouquet_plot' format(x, ...)

title: Format a bouquet_plot for display — format.bouquet_plot
text: x bouquet_plot object returned make_plot_bouquet(). ... Unused; present S3 compatibility.

title: Format a bouquet_plot for display — format.bouquet_plot
text: character string description.

what: Angular Accumulation (Flower Bouquet) Plot for Time Series — make_plot_bouquet
title: Angular Accumulation (Flower Bouquet) Plot for Time Series — make_plot_bouquet
text: Draws time series turtle-graphics path shared origin, encoding binary direction consecutive changes fixed-angle turns: increase turns heading left \(\theta\) decrease turns right \(\theta\). change continues straight ahead. series share origin angle, visual similarity paths directly reflects similarity fluctuation patterns across series.

title: Angular Accumulation (Flower Bouquet) Plot for Time Series — make_plot_bouquet
code: make_plot_bouquet(   data,   time_col = 1,   series_col = 2,   value_col = 3,   stem_colors = "#3a7d2c",   flower_colors = "#f472b6",   facet_by = NULL,   ceiling_pct = 0.8,   launch_deg = 90,   marker_every = NULL,   show_rings = FALSE,   dark_mode = FALSE,   title = NULL,   subtitle = NULL,   hide_legend_after = 10L,   lon_col = NULL,   lat_col = NULL,   map_width = 0.35,   coord_crs = NULL,   cluster_hull = NULL,   hull_coverage = 1,   blend = TRUE,   highlight = NULL,   from = NULL,   to = NULL,   normalise = FALSE,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Angular Accumulation (Flower Bouquet) Plot for Time Series — make_plot_bouquet
text: data long-format data frame tibble minimum time column, series identifier column, numeric value column. Additional factor character columns may referenced stem_colors, flower_colors, facet_by, cluster_hull. time_col <tidy-select> time index column. Defaults first column. series_col <tidy-select> column identifying individual time series. Defaults second column. value_col <tidy-select> numeric value column. Defaults third column. stem_colors Controls colour time series lines. Four options accepted: single hex string – lines share colour. Default: "#3a7d2c" (plant green). character vector hex strings, one per series – recycled warning short. "greens" – distinct shades curated palette natural greens. bare column name data – series coloured column's value using perceptually uniform colours. flower_colors Controls colour end--series flower symbols. four options stem_colors. keyword "blossom". Default: "#f472b6" (warm pink). facet_by <tidy-select> Optional bare column name. supplied plot split via ggplot2::facet_wrap(). NULL (default) = single panel. ceiling_pct single number (0, 1]. Fraction theoretical maximum angle use \(\theta\). Default 0.80. launch_deg Initial heading degrees (counter-clockwise positive x-axis). 90 = straight . Defaults 90. marker_every Positive integer. dot plotted every marker_every-th step. NULL (default) = markers. show_rings Logical. Draw faint concentric reference rings. Default FALSE. dark_mode Logical. TRUE = dark navy background. Default FALSE. title Plot title string. NULL (default) = title. subtitle Subtitle string. NULL (default) = auto-generated summary theta, binding series, time range, interval. hide_legend_after Positive integer. Legend suppressed n_series >= hide_legend_after. Default 10. lon_col <tidy-select> Optional bare column name longitude. Must paired lat_col. provided location map attached right via patchwork. lat_col <tidy-select> Optional bare column name latitude. See lon_col. map_width Relative width map panel fraction combined figure width. used coordinates supplied. Default 0.35. coord_crs Integer EPSG code NULL. CRS lon_col/lat_col. NULL = auto-detect coordinate magnitude. Decimal-degree WGS84 coordinates require reprojection. cluster_hull <tidy-select> Optional bare column name containing cluster labels (e.g. cluster column produced cluster_bouquet()). supplied location map shown (via lon_col/lat_col), smooth concave hull drawn around cluster's map points using ggforce::geom_mark_hull() ggforce package. Hull fill colour matches cluster's flower_colors. NULL (default) = hulls. hull_coverage single number (0, 1]. Fraction cluster's points (closest cluster centroid) included computing hull. Values 1 exclude outlier points hull focuses cluster's core area, useful many clusters overlap. Default 1 = points. blend Logical. TRUE (default) ggblend package installed, cluster hull fills composited "lighten" blend mode map points "multiply", giving perceptually richer colour mixing hulls points overlap. Falls back silently normal alpha blending ggblend available. affects map panel; effect location map shown. highlight character vector series names emphasise. series drawn pale grey low opacity. NULL (default) = series equally prominent. Optional scalar class time_col. Observations value dropped computing paths. NULL = filter. Optional scalar class time_col. Observations value dropped. NULL = filter. normalise Logical. TRUE, series assigned per-series \(\theta\) every series uses full angular range independently volatile . makes shape comparison easier series different volatility. FALSE (default) series share single global \(\theta\) derived volatile series. verbose Logical. Print angle diagnostics console. Default FALSE.

title: Angular Accumulation (Flower Bouquet) Plot for Time Series — make_plot_bouquet
text: bouquet_plot object. lon_col lat_col NULL, underlying object ggplot2::ggplot. coordinates supplied, patchwork object (bouquet left, map right). wrapped bouquet_plot S3 class provides informative print() header format() method. operations work ggplot/patchwork (e.g. ggsave(), +) continue work.

title: Angular Accumulation (Flower Bouquet) Plot for Time Series — make_plot_bouquet
text: data must long format: one row per time step per series. Series share time index values equal length.

title: Angular Accumulation (Flower Bouquet) Plot for Time Series — make_plot_bouquet
text: series, signed first difference binarised \(+1\) (increase), \(-1\) (decrease), \(0\) (change). heading step \(\) accumulates via base::cumsum(); coordinates follow \(x_i = \sum \cos(h_k)\), \(y_i = \sum \sin(h_k)\).

title: Angular Accumulation (Flower Bouquet) Plot for Time Series — make_plot_bouquet
text: $$\theta = \frac{360\degree}{\max_s(\max C_s - \min C_s)} \times   \texttt{ceiling\_pct}$$

title: Angular Accumulation (Flower Bouquet) Plot for Time Series — make_plot_bouquet
code: # \donttest{ set.seed(42) n      <- 52 weeks  <- seq(as.Date("2023-01-01"), by = "week", length.out = n) season <- sin(seq(0, 2 * pi, length.out = n))  gw_long <- tibble::tibble(   week    = rep(weeks, 3),   station = rep(c("Station A", "Station B", "Station C"), each = n),   region  = rep(c("North", "North", "South"), each = n),   level_m = c(     8.5 + 0.8 * season + cumsum(rnorm(n,  0.00, 0.18)),     7.2 + 0.5 * season + cumsum(rnorm(n,  0.02, 0.22)),     9.1 + 1.1 * season + cumsum(rnorm(n, -0.01, 0.15))   ) )  # Minimal call make_plot_bouquet(gw_long,   time_col = week, series_col = station, value_col = level_m) #> <bouquet_plot>  3 series | theta = 11.1 deg | binding: Station C   # Highlight one station; others are dimmed make_plot_bouquet(gw_long,   time_col   = week,   series_col = station,   value_col  = level_m,   highlight  = "Station A") #> <bouquet_plot>  3 series | theta = 11.1 deg | binding: Station C   # Normalised mode: each series uses the full angular range make_plot_bouquet(gw_long,   time_col   = week,   series_col = station,   value_col  = level_m,   normalise  = TRUE) #> <bouquet_plot>  3 series | theta = 11.1 deg | binding: Station C [normalised]  # }

what: Interactive Angular Accumulation Plot (plotly hover tooltips) — make_plot_bouquet_interactive
title: Interactive Angular Accumulation Plot (plotly hover tooltips) — make_plot_bouquet_interactive
text: Builds turtle-graphics bouquet make_plot_bouquet() converts interactive plotly figure. Hovering point path shows series name, date/time, step number, value.

title: Interactive Angular Accumulation Plot (plotly hover tooltips) — make_plot_bouquet_interactive
code: make_plot_bouquet_interactive(   data,   time_col = 1,   series_col = 2,   value_col = 3,   stem_colors = "#3a7d2c",   flower_colors = "#f472b6",   ceiling_pct = 0.8,   launch_deg = 90,   dark_mode = FALSE,   title = NULL,   from = NULL,   to = NULL,   normalise = FALSE,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Interactive Angular Accumulation Plot (plotly hover tooltips) — make_plot_bouquet_interactive
text: data long-format data frame – format make_plot_bouquet(). time_col <tidy-select> Time column. Default: first column. series_col <tidy-select> Series identifier column. Default: second column. value_col <tidy-select> Numeric value column. Default: third column. stem_colors Single hex string named character vector (one hex per series name). Default "#3a7d2c". flower_colors Single hex string named character vector end--series markers. Default "#f472b6". ceiling_pct Fraction maximum angle. Default 0.80. launch_deg Launch heading degrees. Default 90. dark_mode Logical. Default FALSE. title Plot title string. Default NULL. Optional time lower bound filtering. Default NULL. Optional time upper bound filtering. Default NULL. normalise Logical. Per-series angle normalisation. Default FALSE. verbose Logical. Print angle diagnostics. Default FALSE.

title: Interactive Angular Accumulation Plot (plotly hover tooltips) — make_plot_bouquet_interactive
text: plotly htmlwidget object.

what: Plot silhouette scores across k values for a cluster_bouquet result — plot_cluster_quality
title: Plot silhouette scores across k values for a cluster_bouquet result — plot_cluster_quality
text: Draws bar chart composite silhouette score (plain mean silhouette resolution = 0) across candidate k values evaluated automatic k selection. selected k highlighted. Use inspect elbow decide whether different k might preferable.

title: Plot silhouette scores across k values for a cluster_bouquet result — plot_cluster_quality
code: plot_cluster_quality(x, dark_mode = FALSE)

title: Plot silhouette scores across k values for a cluster_bouquet result — plot_cluster_quality
text: x cluster_bouquet object returned cluster_bouquet(). dark_mode Logical. Match dark background make_plot_bouquet(). Default FALSE.

title: Plot silhouette scores across k values for a cluster_bouquet result — plot_cluster_quality
text: ggplot2::ggplot object (invisibly), also printed device.

title: Plot silhouette scores across k values for a cluster_bouquet result — plot_cluster_quality
text: function requires cluster_bouquet() called k = "auto" – fixed k supplied scores plot.

title: Plot silhouette scores across k values for a cluster_bouquet result — plot_cluster_quality
code: # \donttest{ set.seed(42) n <- 52L gw <- tibble::tibble(   week    = rep(seq(as.Date("2023-01-01"), by = "week", length.out = n), 6L),   station = rep(paste0("S", 1:6), each = n),   level   = c(cumsum(rnorm(n)), cumsum(rnorm(n)), cumsum(rnorm(n)),               cumsum(rnorm(n)), cumsum(rnorm(n)), cumsum(rnorm(n))) ) result <- cluster_bouquet(gw, week, station, level) plot_cluster_quality(result)  # }

what: Save a bouquet plot to a file — save_bouquet
title: Save a bouquet plot to a file — save_bouquet
text: thin wrapper around ggplot2::ggsave() sensible defaults square-ish aspect ratio typical bouquet plots, remember right width/height time.

title: Save a bouquet plot to a file — save_bouquet
code: save_bouquet(   plot = ggplot2::last_plot(),   file = "bouquet.png",   width = 10,   height = 8,   units = "in",   dpi = 300,   ... )

title: Save a bouquet plot to a file — save_bouquet
text: plot ggplot patchwork object returned make_plot_bouquet(). Defaults last plot printed (ggplot2::last_plot()). file Path output file. format inferred extension (.png, .pdf, .svg, etc.). Default "bouquet.png". width Width units. Default 10. height Height units. Default 8. units One "", "cm", "mm", "px". Default "". dpi Resolution dots per inch. Used raster formats (PNG, JPEG, TIFF). Default 300. ... Additional arguments forwarded ggplot2::ggsave().

title: Save a bouquet plot to a file — save_bouquet
text: file path, invisibly.

title: Save a bouquet plot to a file — save_bouquet
code: # \donttest{ set.seed(42) n   <- 52L gw  <- tibble::tibble(   week    = rep(seq(as.Date("2023-01-01"), by = "week", length.out = n), 3L),   station = rep(c("A", "B", "C"), each = n),   level   = c(cumsum(rnorm(n)), cumsum(rnorm(n)), cumsum(rnorm(n))) ) p <- make_plot_bouquet(gw, week, station, level) tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".png") save_bouquet(p, tmp) #> <bouquet_plot>  3 series | theta = 14.4 deg | binding: C #> Saved bouquet plot to: /tmp/RtmpNwm26O/file1db04f8b81bd.png # }

title: Summarise a cluster_bouquet result — summary.cluster_bouquet
text: Prints structured report clustering: method, k selection scores (k chosen automatically), silhouette quality, cluster sizes, per-series silhouette widths.

title: Summarise a cluster_bouquet result — summary.cluster_bouquet
code: # S3 method for class 'cluster_bouquet' summary(object, ...)

title: Summarise a cluster_bouquet result — summary.cluster_bouquet
text: object cluster_bouquet object returned cluster_bouquet(). ... Unused; present S3 compatibility.

title: Summarise a cluster_bouquet result — summary.cluster_bouquet
text: object, invisibly (result can still piped).

title: Summarise a cluster_bouquet result — summary.cluster_bouquet
code: set.seed(42) n <- 52L gw <- tibble::tibble(   week    = rep(seq(as.Date("2023-01-01"), by = "week", length.out = n), 4L),   station = rep(paste0("S", 1:4), each = n),   level   = c(cumsum(rnorm(n)), cumsum(rnorm(n)),               cumsum(rnorm(n)), cumsum(rnorm(n))) ) summary(cluster_bouquet(gw, week, station, level)) #> -- cluster_bouquet summary ---------------------------------------------- #>   Method    : pca_hclust #>   Distance  : euclidean (unused) #>   Seed      : 42 #>   Series    : 4   k = 3   Resolution = 0.50 #>   Mean silhouette : 0.072  (weak structure -- consider more data or fewer clusters) #>  #>   Auto k selection (composite silhouette score): #>     k = 2 : 0.0349 #>     k = 3 : 0.0716  <-- selected #>  #>   Cluster sizes and members: #>     C1  (2 series, mean sil = 0.143) #>        S1, S4 #>     C2  (1 series, mean sil = 0.000) #>        S2 #>     C3  (1 series, mean sil = 0.000) #>        S3 #>  #>   Per-series silhouette widths: #>     S4                    C1   0.158  ||| #>     S1                    C1   0.129  ||| #>     S2                    C2   0.000   #>     S3                    C3   0.000   #> ------------------------------------------------------------------------
